channel.title: Business Automation on Bush Digital Guides
channel.description: Recent content in Business Automation on Bush Digital Guides

item.title: How to Automate Your Small Business in Australia: A Practical 2026 Guide
item.description:
If you’re running a small business in Australia, repetitive tasks may include chasing invoices, scheduling appointments, answering common customer questions, posting on social media, and reconciling books. Some of these tasks can be supported by carefully configured software.

Many automation tools are designed for small teams and do not require custom software development. You still need a clear process, appropriate access controls, and a plan for checking the output.

This guide describes implementation choices for common business processes and Australian-oriented tool selection.

item.title: How to Automate Invoice Chasing for Small Businesses in Australia
item.description:
Let’s be real for a second. Repeatedly checking overdue invoices and writing follow-up messages can be an awkward part of running a business. Late payments can also make cash-flow planning and supplier commitments harder.

You can support invoice chasing with scheduled reminders and review tasks. This guide walks through the practical setup — from built-in tools in Xero and MYOB through to workflows using Zapier and Make.

Why Late Payments Need a Clear Process

Before we get into the how, let’s talk about why this matters beyond the obvious “I want my money.”
